summary: This PR removed a mistakenly emitted code label at address 0x80008FF8 and changed two data-table entries from symbolic references to lbl_80008FF8 into raw .4byte constants 0x80008FF8. The change prevents the assembler/decomp symbol map from treating an instruction inside func_80008FC8 as a real pointer target or global label.
what_changed: In asm/data.s, two occurrences of `.4byte lbl_80008FF8` under repeated lbl_8040FF80 data regions were replaced with `.4byte 0x80008FF8`.
In asm/melee/lb/code_80008D30.s, the `.global lbl_80008FF8` declaration and `lbl_80008FF8:` label were removed.
The instruction at 0x80008FF8 remains part of func_80008FC8: `addi r31, r7, lbl_803B9880@l`; only the artificial label before it was removed.
smart_moves: Preserved the exact 32-bit value in data.s while removing the symbolic relocation-like reference, which keeps binary data intact without implying that the value is a genuine pointer.
Removed the global label at an instruction boundary inside func_80008FC8 rather than renaming it, avoiding creation of a misleading code symbol.
Made a minimal assembly-only correction: two data edits and two label-line deletions, reducing risk to unrelated matching state.
decomp_lessons: Not every 0x80000000-range value in raw data should become a symbolic pointer; some values may be immediate data that only coincidentally look like addresses.
False labels can cause future decomp work to infer incorrect function boundaries, jump targets, or pointer tables.
When a data word must remain numerically identical but should not be treated as a symbol reference, use a raw `.4byte 0x...` constant instead of a label.
Labels inside an existing function should have clear evidence such as branch targets, table references, or real relocation needs before being promoted to global symbols.
naming_conventions: The removed synthetic label used the default address-based form `lbl_80008FF8`.
The affected function retained the default address-based name `func_80008FC8`.
No new descriptive C or assembly symbol names were introduced.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Convert suspect `.4byte lbl_...` entries to literal hex constants when the value should not emit or depend on a symbol reference.
Delete `.global` plus local label definitions for false positives inside function bodies to keep symbol boundaries cleaner.
Keep the original instruction stream unchanged when the issue is metadata/symbolization rather than code bytes.
follow_up_queries: Search for remaining `.4byte lbl_80008FF8` references to verify the false pointer was fully removed.
Audit other address-looking values in asm/data.s for mistaken symbolic labels.
Check whether labels inside func_80008FC8 have branch or relocation evidence before preserving them as globals.
